title: Avoid multiple spaces in regular expressions
description: Regular expressions containing multiple consecutive spaces are often a sign of mistakes or unclear intent. Such patterns can lead to unexpected matches or make the regex harder to read and maintain. This rule helps ensure regular expressions are concise and unambiguous.
recommendation: Review and refactor regular expressions to avoid using multiple consecutive spaces unless explicitly required.

noncompliant: Pattern.compile("foo  bar"); // Two spaces between 'foo' and 'bar'
compliant: Pattern.compile("foo bar"); // Single space between 'foo' and 'bar'

checks.message: Use \s+ or \s* instead of multiple consecutive spaces in regular expressions.
